trigger: shipper asks for exact sailing or booking readiness
logic: Do not infer exact departure from static carrier profile. A live carrier schedule lookup is required for origin, destination, date window, cargo type and equipment.

trigger: live carrier schedule search returns no service for port pair/date window
logic: Shipment cannot be booked on that carrier/service for that window without alternative routing.
Carrier DG acceptance is required even if SEA-A regulatory reference says transport may be possible.

trigger: cargo is out-of-gauge, flat-rack, open-top, breakbulk, project cargo, or heavy lift
logic: Special cargo desk/carrier approval is required before booking.
Carrier must offer reefer equipment/service on the route and terminal must support reefer handling.

trigger: origin/destination likely not both mainline hub ports
logic: Feeder/transshipment route may add delay, missed-connection risk, extra handling and documentation timing risk.

trigger: user asks cost or space availability
logic: Static carrier reference does not provide rate or space. Requires quote/API/TMS/carrier booking data.
